concerns.text: FR-008 mandates the entire pipeline run within 6 hours on a CPU-only environment. The upstream idea explicitly states an expected total wall-time of ≤ 45 minutes. The spec's 6-hour constraint is a significant relaxation (8x) that contradicts the idea's performance expectation and may indicate a drift in feasibility assumptions or a misunderstanding of the computational load.

User Story 2 and FR-003 specify computing 'multiscale sample entropy (MSE)' but the implementation details only mention configurable parameters (m, r) typical of standard Sample Entropy. Multiscale Entropy requires computing entropy across multiple temporal scales (coarse-graining). The spec lacks explicit requirements for the scale factor range or the coarse-graining procedure, risking implementation of standard Sample Entropy instead of the requested Multiscale variant.

The idea's methodology sketch explicitly lists three robustness checks: (i) alternative entropy parameters, (ii) reverse-causality testing (swapping predictor/predicted), and (iii) motion-exclusion verification. The spec includes (i) in FR-007 and (iii) in Edge Cases, but completely omits the 'reverse-causality' check. This is an under-reach of the explicitly requested robustness analysis.
